inputs.name: accept
inputs.description:
Comma-separated list of file types that the component should accept.

*Note:** Please make sure you are adding the `.` in front on the file type, e.g. `.png` in case you want to accept png's only.

inputs.name: accessibleDescription
inputs.description: Defines the accessible description of the component.

inputs.name: accessibleDescriptionRef
inputs.description: Receives id(or many ids) of the elements that describe the input.

inputs.name: accessibleName
inputs.description: Defines the accessible ARIA name of the component.

inputs.name: accessibleNameRef
inputs.description: Receives id(or many ids) of the elements that label the input.

inputs.name: disabled
inputs.description:
Defines whether the component is in disabled state.

*Note:** A disabled component is completely noninteractive.

inputs.name: hideInput
inputs.description:
If set to "true", the input field of component will not be rendered. Only the default slot that is passed will be rendered.

*Note:** Use this property in combination with the default slot to achieve a button-only file uploader design.

inputs.name: maxFileSize
inputs.description: Defines the maximum file size in megabytes which prevents the upload if at least one file exceeds it.

inputs.name: multiple
inputs.description: Allows multiple files to be chosen.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description:
Determines the name by which the component will be identified upon submission in an HTML form.

*Note:** This property is only applicable within the context of an HTML Form element.

inputs.name: placeholder
inputs.description: Defines a short hint intended to aid the user with data entry when the component has no value.

inputs.name: required
inputs.description: Defines whether the component is required.

inputs.name: value
inputs.description: Defines the name/names of the file/files to upload.

inputs.name: valueState
inputs.description: Defines the value state of the component.

outputs.name: ui5Change
outputs.description:
Event is fired when the value of the file path has been changed.

*Note:** Keep in mind that because of the HTML input element of type file, the event is also fired in Chrome browser when the Cancel button of the uploads window is pressed.

outputs.name: ui5FileSizeExceed
outputs.description: Event is fired when the size of a file is above the `maxFileSize` property value.
